scope.purpose: Enumerate and classify all merge-capable code admission paths into the main branch. Classification only — does not authorize, deny, or modify any merge operation.

classification_key.GOVERNED: Merge path requires PREO_VALID, required status checks passing, and review binding. Fail-closed on legitimacy gap.
classification_key.PARTIAL: Merge path has partial governance controls. Legitimacy gap possible under certain conditions.
classification_key.BREAK_GLASS: Emergency path with root authority. Visible and classified. Cannot produce PREO_VALID or merge legitimacy.
classification_key.UNKNOWN: Path existence or governance status is not determined from repository state alone.

surfaces.name: Standard Pull Request Merge
surfaces.description: Code admitted via GitHub PR flow: branch → PR → review → status checks → merge
governance_controls: PREO_CANDIDATE generated per merge-governance-check workflow
SCO_CANDIDATE generated for governed-path mutations
Required status checks: merge-governance-check, merge-guard, generate-preo-candidate, generate-sco-candidate, constitutional-integrity
Minimum 1 approving review required
Stale review dismissal enabled
Branch must be up-to-date before merge
PREO/SCO bound to exact PR head_sha
surfaces.bypass_risk: LOW — requires all required checks to pass and PREO_VALID
surfaces.gap: Branch protection enforcement status is advisory; actual GitHub branch protection rule activation is external to repository code.

surfaces.name: Squash Merge via Pull Request
surfaces.description: PR merged using GitHub squash merge method — rewrites commit history into single commit
governance_controls: PREO/SCO remain bound to exact PR head_sha
Proof must bind resulting merge_commit_sha (different from head_sha)
All required status checks must pass against current head_sha before squash
Stale review or check evidence cannot be reused
surfaces.legitimacy_source: PREO_VALID plus merge_commit_sha binding in proof
surfaces.bypass_risk: LOW — same PREO/SCO binding requirements as standard merge
surfaces.gap: Merge commit SHA diverges from reviewed head_sha — proof must explicitly record both to preserve exact-object discipline.

surfaces.name: Rebase Merge via Pull Request
surfaces.description: PR merged using GitHub rebase merge method — replays commits onto base branch
governance_controls: PREO/SCO remain bound to exact PR head_sha
Proof must bind resulting merge_commit_sha
All required status checks must pass against current head_sha before rebase
Stale review or check evidence cannot be reused
surfaces.legitimacy_source: PREO_VALID plus merge_commit_sha binding in proof
surfaces.bypass_risk: LOW — same PREO/SCO binding requirements as standard merge
surfaces.gap: Merge commits produced by rebase diverge from reviewed head_sha — same exact-object gap as squash merge.

surfaces.name: Merge Queue
surfaces.description: GitHub merge queue orchestrates batched or serialized merges after all required checks resolve against enqueued head_sha
governance_controls: Required checks must resolve against enqueued head_sha — not stale PR head_sha
PREO/SCO binding requirement applies to the enqueued commit, not the original PR head_sha
Merge queue does not regenerate PREO/SCO automatically — gap exists if checks run against queue-combined SHA
surfaces.legitimacy_source: PREO_VALID — only if required checks run against current enqueued head_sha and PREO remains bound
surfaces.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — merge queue may merge against a combined SHA that was never reviewed as-is; PREO binding to original PR head_sha may not cover the enqueued object
surfaces.gap: Merge queue may introduce an unreviewed combined SHA. PREO/SCO workflows are PR-event-triggered and do not re-execute against the enqueued merge SHA. This is a partial governance gap requiring merge-queue-specific PREO regeneration to close.

surfaces.name: Auto-Merge
surfaces.description: GitHub auto-merge automatically merges a PR once all required checks and review conditions are satisfied
governance_controls: Auto-merge respects required status checks and required review counts
Will not merge if any required check fails or review requirement is unmet
surfaces.legitimacy_source: PREO_VALID — inherited from the PR merge flow if all conditions are satisfied at merge time
surfaces.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — auto-merge executes merge automatically after conditions are satisfied; temporal gap between review approval and merge execution creates stale-approval risk if push occurs between approval and auto-merge trigger
surfaces.gap: Auto-merge creates a timing window between approval and merge execution. If a push occurs after approval but before auto-merge fires, the stale-review-dismissal setting determines whether the merge is blocked. Auto-merge policy compliance depends on GitHub branch protection enforcement, which is external to repository code.

surfaces.name: Bot Merge via Workflow Token
surfaces.description: Automated bot or GitHub Actions workflow that opens and merges a PR using a GITHUB_TOKEN or PAT
governance_controls: If bot PR passes through standard PR merge flow with required checks, PREO/SCO are generated
Bot identity must be a recognized merge actor — unrecognized bot actors produce UNKNOWN merge lineage
surfaces.legitimacy_source: PREO_VALID — only if bot PR passes through governed PR merge path
surfaces.bypass_risk: HIGH — a bot with write access and sufficient GitHub token permissions may be able to create a PR and merge it programmatically; if the bot bypasses required checks via admin scope, the merge becomes an ungoverned path
surfaces.gap: Bot merge actors are not explicitly enumerated or restricted in the repository's branch protection policy. Bot identity and authority scope are not validated by the PREO/SCO workflows. Requires explicit bot actor classification and authority containment.

surfaces.name: Workflow-Dispatched Merge
surfaces.description: A GitHub Actions workflow dispatched manually or by another workflow that performs a merge operation
governance_controls: governed-deploy.yml requires invocation_nonce and validated_object_hash inputs
governed-release.yml requires release_tag, source_commit_sha, and pr_number inputs
No workflow in the repository performs a direct git merge operation against main
surfaces.legitimacy_source: Governed workflows (governed-deploy, governed-release) are not merge surfaces — they operate against already-merged code
surfaces.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — workflow_dispatch access is governed by GitHub permissions; a workflow with write:contents permission could be modified to perform a direct push or merge
surfaces.gap: Workflow dispatch paths with write:contents permission represent potential mutation surfaces. No workflow currently performs a direct merge — but a modified or new workflow could. Requires workflow-mutation SCO classification to govern.

surfaces.name: Direct Push to main
surfaces.description: A collaborator with write access pushes directly to the main branch without opening a PR
governance_controls: Branch protection policy declares direct push to main as forbidden
MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json classifies direct_push_to_main as a bypass path
Classification: source_control_mutation_bypass — result: MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
surfaces.legitimacy_source: none — direct push cannot produce PREO_VALID
surfaces.bypass_risk: HIGH — requires write access to the repository; produces root authority evidence only
surfaces.gap: Enforcement depends on GitHub branch protection rules being active. Repository code declares the policy but cannot enforce it without active branch protection. Current enforcement_classification is advisory.

surfaces.name: Force Push to main
surfaces.description: A collaborator with admin or write access rewrites main branch history via force push
governance_controls: Branch protection policy declares allow_force_pushes: false
MERGE_GOVERNANCE_RULES.json classifies force_push_to_main as bypass — result: MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
Result: ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED
surfaces.legitimacy_source: none — force push cannot produce PREO_VALID
surfaces.bypass_risk: CRITICAL — rewrites commit history; erases prior merge lineage
surfaces.gap: Same as direct push — requires active GitHub branch protection enforcement. Force push may also erase prior proof and lineage bindings.

surfaces.name: Admin Override / Branch Protection Bypass
surfaces.description: Repository admin bypasses branch protection rules to merge without required checks or reviews
governance_controls: BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json declares allow_admin_bypass: false
Admin bypass classified as root_authority_evidence_only — not merge legitimacy
Result: ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED -> NULL
surfaces.legitimacy_source: none — admin bypass cannot produce PREO_VALID
surfaces.bypass_risk: CRITICAL — undetectable from within repository code alone; requires external audit logging
surfaces.gap: Admin bypass cannot be prevented by repository code alone. Requires GitHub org-level policy enforcement or an independent audit log outside the repository.

surfaces.name: Dependabot or External Bot Auto-Merge
surfaces.description: A dependency update bot (Dependabot, Renovate, etc.) opens and auto-merges a PR using elevated permissions
governance_controls: No explicit Dependabot or Renovate configuration is present in the repository
If enabled, bot PRs would pass through the standard PR merge flow — but bot review approval may short-circuit required human review
surfaces.legitimacy_source: Conditionally PREO_VALID — only if required checks pass and human review is not bypassed
surfaces.bypass_risk: MEDIUM — auto-merging dependency updates without human review can introduce ungoverned mutations to runtime or governance paths
surfaces.gap: Bot auto-merge policy for external dependency updaters is not declared. Requires explicit classification and authority containment if these surfaces are ever activated.

surfaces_requiring_gap_closure: MS-004 — Merge Queue: PREO/SCO not regenerated against enqueued SHA
MS-006 — Bot Merge: bot actor not classified or authority-bound
MS-007 — Workflow-Dispatch Merge: write:contents workflow mutation surface not enumerated
MS-008 — Direct Push: enforcement external to repository
MS-009 — Force Push: enforcement external to repository
MS-010 — Admin Override: enforcement external to repository
MS-011 — External Bot Auto-Merge: not configured, not assessed
summary.single_governed_path_status: NOT_YET_ACHIEVED — multiple partial and break-glass paths remain open or unenforced
